chunk_summary: Low-budget vegetarian dietary strategies for Indian children focus on protein sufficiency, reducing refined carbohydrates, and managing school food choices to mitigate metabolic risks like high triglycerides and insulin resistance.
learning_objectives: Understand the importance of protein sufficiency in low-budget vegetarian diets for children.
Identify practical strategies to reduce refined carbohydrate and sugar intake in school and home settings.
Learn how to set measurable dietary goals to improve metabolic health in children.
Recognize the role of school food environments in influencing dietary adherence.
key_facts: Protein sufficiency is critical to prevent high triglycerides (TG) and insulin resistance in vegetarian diets.
Affordable protein sources include dal, rajma, chana, soy, curd, milk, groundnuts, and eggs (if acceptable).
School snacks should avoid sweet beverages and fried bakery items; a single snack rule is recommended.
Weekly goal: 5 school days with no sugar-sweetened drinks and no more than one packaged snack.
Improvement in fasting TG or ALT can serve as a measurable outcome of dietary adherence.
reference_quote: Diet prescriptions fail when they ignore budget and the school food environment. For low-budget vegetarian adolescents, focus on protein sufficiency at each meal to prevent compensatory high refined-carbohydrate intake that worsens TG and insulin resistance.
document_key_topics: School food environment and metabolic risk
Practical dietary goals for Indian families
